query: Tesla Cybercab unboxed process parallel assembly manufacturing implementation details
a_issues: Relies heavily on unverified social-media posts (LinkedIn, Facebook) for its core module list (front/rear mega-casting, structural pack, full interior, painted plastic panels) and cost/cycle-time figures, which are not corroborated by mainstream reporting and slightly diverge from the module breakdown given in more authoritative sources (e.g., left/right side structures are omitted).
b_issues: Includes one source (cybercabrobotaxiforum) that casts doubt on whether the unboxed line is truly parallel, creating a mild internal contradiction with the other sources' descriptions of parallel module assembly; also mixes some speculative future dates (e.g., April 2026 production start) that conflict slightly with other reports.
reasoning: Content B draws from more credible outlets (Forbes, InsideEVs, Basenor) and its Forbes excerpt almost exactly mirrors the anchor's authoritative description of the five parallel modules (front end, structural battery pack + interior, rear end, left/right side structures), plus concrete figures like 40% footprint reduction and cycle-time comparisons (5-10s vs 33s). Content A's key details come mainly from a LinkedIn post and Facebook post, which are less reliable and give a different, unverified module breakdown. Despite a minor contradictory note in B about the line's appearance, its overall factual alignment with the anchor and use of more reputable sources make it better suited to answer the question accurately.
